title: useSortedTypeFields

import { Tabs, TabItem } from '@astrojs/starlight/components';
import EditorAction from "@/components/EditorAction.astro";

<Tabs>
<TabItem label="GraphQL" icon="seti:graphql">
:::note
This rule has been implemented but not released yet. It will be available in the next release.
:::
## Summary
- Diagnostic Category: [`assist/source/useSortedTypeFields`](/reference/diagnostics#diagnostic-category)
- Sources: 
  - Inspired from [`@graphql-eslint/alphabetize`](https://the-guild.dev/graphql/eslint/rules/alphabetize)

## How to enable in your editor
<EditorAction includeFixAll action="source.action.useSortedTypeFields.biome" />
## How to configure
```json title="biome.json"
{
	"assist": {
		"actions": {
			"source": {
				"useSortedTypeFields": "on"
			}
		}
	}
}

```
## Description
Sort fields in GraphQL type definitions alphabetically.

This rule ensures that fields within `type`, `interface`, and `input`
definitions are sorted alphabetically. For GraphQL identifiers (`[A-Za-z0-9_]`),
the sort order matches JavaScript's `localeCompare()`, including case handling.

## Examples

```graphql
type User {
  name: String
  age: Int
  id: ID
}
```
